title: carex divulsa var. contigua
intro: Carex divulsa var. contigua, also known as contiguous sedge, is a perennial plant species in the sedge family (Cyperaceae), characterized by its slender stems and dark brown scales. Traditionally, Carex divulsa var. contigua has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Carex divulsa var. contigua can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to address various health issues.
